landing.title: The Algerian Platform for Source Code Archiving
landing.heroSubtitle: The national platform for archiving, preserving, and sharing source code. Ensuring digital sovereignty and fueling academic excellence for the next generation of Algerian developers.
landing.whyAscapTitle: Building a sovereign digital ecosystem for Algerian innovation.
landing.whyAscapSubtitle: We provide the infrastructure needed to keep Algerian intellectual property secure and accessible within national borders.
landing.featureSovereigntyDesc: Keep your code hosted securely within national borders. Ensure full compliance with Algerian data protection laws and sovereignty requirements.
landing.featureLocalInfraDesc: Experience low-latency access and high reliability powered by local Algerian data centers. No reliance on foreign servers.
landing.featureAcademicDesc: Seamlessly integrated with university workflows to support Decision 1275 startup projects, thesis archiving, and research collaboration.
landing.networkSubtitle: From Oran to Annaba, Algiers to Ouargla. A unified network for the nation's code.
landing.ctaSubtitle: Join thousands of Algerian developers, students, and researchers securing their intellectual property today.
landing.footerTagline: The Algerian Source Code Archiving Platform. Empowering digital sovereignty through secure, local infrastructure.
landing.footerCopyright: © 2026 ASCAP. All rights reserved. Ministry of Higher Education and Scientific Research.
landing.tagline: Secure your code in a national, sovereign environment. Archive, share, and collaborate. Preserving Algeria's digital heritage.
landing.whatIsAscapDesc: The national code archiving platform for Algeria—an alternative to GitHub/GitLab that keeps your code on sovereign infrastructure. We support open science, academic projects, and compliance with Ministerial Decree 1275 (Diploma – Startup – Patent).
landing.feature2Desc: Upload any language; node_modules and .env are excluded.
landing.feature3Desc: View code with syntax highlighting for many languages.
landing.feature4Desc: Browse public repos and search by name or description.
landing.feature5Desc: A public page per user showing their public repos.
landing.feature6Desc: View README and folder tree with raw and download links.
landing.step1: Create an account and log in.
landing.step2: From the dashboard, create a repo and upload your files or folders.
landing.step3: Share the link or make the repo public to appear in Explore.

about.heroDesc: The Algerian Platform for Archiving Source Code (ASCAP) is the cornerstone of our nation's strategy to preserve intellectual property and ensure compliance with Ministerial Decree 1275.
about.centralizedDesc: A unified repository for all academic and governmental software assets, preventing data fragmentation.
about.decreeDesc: Full technical compliance framework for the Diploma - Startup - Patent mechanism.
about.sovereigntyDesc: Ensuring that critical national code remains hosted within Algerian borders on secure infrastructure.
about.heritageP1: In an era where software defines the capabilities of nations, the preservation of source code is not merely a technical task—it is a matter of national security and cultural heritage. ASCAP was founded with a singular vision: to create a resilient, sovereign digital infrastructure.
about.heritageP2: Our platform serves as the definitive bridge between the academic sector and the industrial startup ecosystem. By archiving the output of thousands of university projects, we prevent the loss of innovation and facilitate the transfer of technology as mandated by Decree 1275.
about.heritageQuote: Digital sovereignty is not an option, but a necessity for the modern state. ASCAP is our answer to the challenge of data independence.
about.roadmapSubtitle: Our strategic phases for national deployment and integration.
about.phase1Desc: Connecting 50+ universities and research centers to the centralized ASCAP repository.
about.phase2Desc: Implementing immutable ledgers to certify the timestamp and authorship of archived code.
about.phase3Desc: Opening controlled APIs for industrial partners to discover and license academic IP.
about.ctaDesc: Join researchers and developers contributing to Algeria's digital sovereignty.
about.ctaDescStats: Join over 12,000 researchers and developers contributing to Algeria's digital sovereignty.
about.leadershipSubtitle: The experts driving the national archiving initiative.
about.whoWeAreIntro: The Algerian Platform for Source Code Archiving is an innovative national digital platform developed in line with the latest international standards while respecting local Algerian specificities. It aims to provide Algerian developers—researchers, programmers, and creators—with a secure and trusted space to preserve and organize their source code. The platform offers an advanced environment that facilitates software project preservation and enables controlled sharing in a secure context with full data ownership. Available features include version management, code documentation, and collaborative teamwork. This initiative is the result of the vision of students Abbad Nassira and Briksi Niqassa Manal from the Archival Science Department at Djillali Liabes University of Sidi Bel Abbes, who identified the major gap in a dedicated Algerian digital space for local developers and launched this startup project. The project was supervised by Dr. Damouch Oussama, Lecturer A at Djillali Liabes University of Sidi Bel Abbes. The platform also seeks to protect source code against all forms of damage and loss, making it a pillar for safeguarding emerging digital heritage as part of humanity's digital cultural heritage.
projectGoals: Provide a secure and reliable environment for preserving and organizing Algerian developers' source code, with data protection aligned with local legal standards.

Encourage knowledge exchange and collaborative work between developers, programmers, and researchers across software domains to improve source code quality and build innovative applications and software.
Preserve the knowledge embedded in source code and software as part of intellectual heritage, and provide a platform that keeps this knowledge available for future generations.
Contribute to strengthening Algeria's digital sovereignty by ensuring local data hosting and reducing dependence on external platforms.
projectAspects: Archive and preserve source code in a locally secure environment according to modern technical standards and global best practices, while providing a local alternative to GitHub and GitLab tailored to the Algerian market.
Strengthen data protection and Algeria's digital sovereignty by hosting the platform on local servers instead of relying on external platforms that may not meet local security expectations.
Support open-source software and national projects by providing an environment that encourages open technical initiatives that improve Algeria's digital ecosystem.
Offer competitive pricing and local support that make platform services more accessible to Algerian developers and improve local technical assistance.
Contribute to building a strong local digital environment by supporting startup initiatives and technical solutions that serve Algerian society and its needs.
about.footerOrg: Ministry of Higher Education and Scientific Research. People's Democratic Republic of Algeria.

institutions.eduDesc: Universities and public research institutions can use ASCAP at no cost for archiving academic projects and theses. Open-source projects are welcome.

certificate.title: Code Preservation Certificate
certificate.certifyText: This certifies that the source code of the above repository has been preserved on the Algerian Platform for Source Code Archiving (ASCAP). The snapshot is retained for research, academic, and compliance purposes in line with national digital sovereignty and Ministerial Decree 1275.
certificate.footerTagline: ASCAP — المنصة الجزائرية لأرشفة الشفرات المصدرية

certificateCenter.subtitle: View and manage your code preservation certificates issued by ASCAP. Verify authenticity and download official documentation.
certificateCenter.verifyDesc: Ensure the authenticity of any ASCAP code preservation record. Enter the unique Certificate ID found on the document.
certificateCenter.verificationNote: Verification requests are logged for security purposes. Access public ledger for bulk verification.

pricing.heroSubtitle: Secure, compliant, and locally hosted source code repositories for students, professionals, and government institutions.
pricing.sectionSubtitle: Choose the perfect plan for your development needs.
pricing.planStudentDesc: Perfect for learning and personal projects.
pricing.whySubtitle: Built for local needs, ensuring your data stays within Algerian borders with full compliance and state-of-the-art security.
pricing.whyLocalDesc: Data centers located in Algiers and Oran, ensuring low latency and data sovereignty. 24/7 support available in Arabic and French.
pricing.whySecurityDesc: Fully compliant with Algerian data protection laws (Law 18-07) and international security standards like ISO 27001.
pricing.whySovereigntyDesc: Ensuring national code assets remain under national jurisdiction, protecting intellectual property from foreign interference.
pricing.ctaTitle: Ready to secure your code in Algeria?
pricing.ctaSubtitle: Join thousands of Algerian developers, startups, and institutions protecting their digital assets today.

api.description: REST API for projects, certificates, and search. Authenticate with your API key.
api.complianceNoteDesc: Projects must adhere to Decision 1275 guidelines. Ensure all contributors are listed correctly.

docs.title: Documentation
docs.archiving1275Desc: A complete guide on how to properly format, upload, and archive your academic project source code to comply with Ministerial Decision 1275.
docs.prerequisitesDesc: Before you begin the archiving process on the ASCAP platform, ensure your project meets the following criteria:
docs.prereq1: You have a valid student or researcher account on ASCAP.
docs.prereq2: Your project source code is hosted on a Git repository (local or remote).
docs.prereq3: You have generated a README.md file explaining the project structure.
docs.complianceNoticeDesc: Ensure no sensitive data (API keys, passwords, database credentials) is included in your source code commits. The platform scans for secrets automatically and will reject insecure submissions.
docs.step1Desc: To ensure long-term preservation, we recommend structuring your repository cleanly. Use a .gitignore file to exclude build artifacts and temporary files.
docs.step2Desc: Once your code is ready, add the ASCAP remote repository URL to your local git configuration. You can find your specific repository URL in your dashboard.
docs.step3Desc: After a successful push, the system will automatically run static analysis. Once passed, navigate to the Certificates tab in your dashboard to download your official deposit proof.
